checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 income statement, prior year comparative
10-K FY2014 income statement, prior year comparative

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2014-11-07 cash flow and income statements

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=188336729.45 (2012-06-30) growing to InventoryNet=323509042.82 (2014-06-30), a ~72% rise vs Revenues rising ~56% (1392268463.60 to 2175520554.86) over same span

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly rising steadily (e.g., 16552701.40, 31943703.38, 41129210.15, 27936345.49, 41650459.62, 35599520.93, 19046819.53) while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities swings widely (27431258.79, 54153375.84, 2640593.28)
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2014-11-07 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Multiple CORRESP/UPLOAD entries in 2009, 2011-2012, and 2014 indicating SEC comment-letter review cycles (e.g., CORRESP 2009-03-06, 2011-11-15, 2012-01-30, 2014-02-12, 2014-03-07)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly series from FY2009 (2008-07-01) through Q1 FY2015 (2014-09-30) across Revenues, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, Liabilities, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad debt reserve (allowance for doubtful accounts) not kept pace with rapidly growing gross receivables balance, especially in the two most recent quarters before the cutoff, potentially understating the bad-debt provision expense and overstating net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2590084.61 (2013-06-30) / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=236017924.29 (2013-06-30) = 1.10% coverage, declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1602135.02 (2014-06-30) / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=290844075.63 (2014-06-30) = 0.55% coverage

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables growth outpacing revenue growth in FY2013 could reflect either aggressive revenue recognition/channel stuffing at quarter/year end or receivables acquired via M&A being consolidated without commensurate revenue contribution, inflating current assets relative to sales.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=168372672.47 (2012-06-30) grew to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=236017924.29 (2013-06-30), +40.2%, versus Revenues growth of +25.9% (1392268463.60 to 1752330631.05) over the same fiscal year

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Aggressive debt-funded acquisition strategy has driven intangible/goodwill balances up far faster than revenue, raising the risk that acquired intangible assets are carried at values not yet tested for impairment adequately, potentially overstating non-current soft assets on the balance sheet.

rationale_evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill grew from 209493801.22 (2011-06-30) to 658109789.61 (2014-06-30), a +214% increase, versus Revenues growth of +94% (1119823686.36 to 2175520554.86) over the same period, while LongTermDebt grew from 231875203.16 (2011-06-30) to 795640857.88 (2014-09-30)

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts as % of gross receivables dropped from ~1.1%-1.6% (2012-2013) to ~0.55%-0.60% (mid-to-late 2014), despite receivables more than doubling since 2011
Accounts receivable grew 40.2% in FY2013 vs revenue growth of only 25.9%, a notable working-capital divergence
Intangible assets (ex-goodwill) grew 214% from FY2011 to FY2014 versus 94% revenue growth, reflecting an aggressive, debt-funded acquisition strategy with associated impairment risk
Most recent quarter (Q1 FY2015) shows operating cash flow of only $2.64M versus net income of $19.05M, a sharp reversal from the prior-year quarter's pattern of CFO exceeding NI

History of SEC comment-letter correspondence (CORRESP/UPLOAD filings) in 2009, 2011-2012, and 2014, plus earlier NT 10-K/NT 10-Q and multiple 10-K/A amendments, indicating recurring disclosure/review issues
